siteConfig.title: Tarang 2026
siteConfig.tagline: The Spectrum of Technology and Culture

departments.name: Civil Engineering
departments.description: The Civil Engineering department brings structural innovation and sustainable building challenges to Tarang 2026.

departmentEvents.title: Civil Brain Challenge
departmentEvents.subtitle: Technical Quiz
departmentEvents.desc: A technical quiz on core Civil Engineering concepts and problem-solving skills.

departmentEvents.title: AutoCAD Home Planner
departmentEvents.subtitle: CAD Drafting Competition
departmentEvents.desc: Drafting a residential building's plan, elevation, and section using AutoCAD.

departmentEvents.title: Paper Tower Challenge
departmentEvents.subtitle: Structural Building Contest
departmentEvents.desc: Build the tallest stable, self-supporting tower using only newspapers and staples.

departmentEvents.title: Stick Tech Bridge
departmentEvents.subtitle: Bridge Building Competition
departmentEvents.desc: Construct a bridge model from popsicle sticks and glue to hold maximum load.

departmentEvents.title: Workshop on SketchUp
departmentEvents.subtitle: 3D Modelling Workshop
departmentEvents.desc: A one-day workshop on 3D house modelling, plugins, and rendering using SketchUp.

departmentEvents.title: Project Competition
departmentEvents.subtitle: Innovation Beyond Boundaries
departmentEvents.desc: Interdisciplinary project competition open to all branches showcasing innovative engineering solutions.

departmentEvents.title: Seminar Competition
departmentEvents.subtitle: Sustainable Development in the 21st Century
departmentEvents.desc: Exploring innovative strategies and technologies driving sustainable development in the 21st century.

departments.name: Electrical & Electronics
departments.description: The Electrical & Electronics department powers up Tarang with electrifying competitions in circuits, wiring, CAD, and Arduino programming.

departmentEvents.title: Electrical Quiz
departmentEvents.subtitle: Technical Quiz
departmentEvents.desc: A curriculum-based quiz for Electrical Engineering students to test core technical knowledge and concepts.

departmentEvents.title: Network Puzzle
departmentEvents.subtitle: Circuit Logic Challenge
departmentEvents.desc: Timed logical and technical puzzles based on basic electric circuits.

departmentEvents.title: Electrical Wiring Competition
departmentEvents.subtitle: Practical Wiring Challenge
departmentEvents.desc: Complete the electrical wiring task as per the given conditions within the allotted time.

departmentEvents.title: Electrical CAD Competition
departmentEvents.subtitle: CAD Design Challenge
departmentEvents.desc: Design accurate electrical layouts and schematics in CAD.

departmentEvents.title: Arduino Programming Workshop
departmentEvents.subtitle: Hands-on Workshop
departmentEvents.desc: Learn Arduino basics through hands-on coding, sensor interfacing, and building real projects.

departmentEvents.title: Project Competition
departmentEvents.subtitle: Innovation Beyond Boundaries
departmentEvents.desc: Develop innovative projects showcasing interdisciplinary engineering solutions. Open to all branches.

departmentEvents.title: Seminar Competition
departmentEvents.subtitle: Sustainable Development in the 21st Century
departmentEvents.desc: Exploring innovative strategies and technologies driving sustainable development in the 21st century.

departments.name: Electronics Engineering
departments.description: The Electronics Engineering department brings cutting-edge digital challenges — from soldering and PCB design to Python debugging.

departmentEvents.title: Hot Joint
departmentEvents.subtitle: Solder & Build
departmentEvents.desc: Assemble a given circuit on a general-purpose PCB using proper soldering techniques.

departmentEvents.title: BugSlayer.py
departmentEvents.subtitle: Hunt, Fix, Win
departmentEvents.desc: A timed contest focused on debugging and optimizing Python programs.

departmentEvents.title: Trace Race
departmentEvents.subtitle: PCB Design Competition
departmentEvents.desc: PCB Designing competition using Proteus 8 — Circuit schematic + PCB Layout design.

departmentEvents.title: Project Competition
departmentEvents.subtitle: Innovation Beyond Boundaries
departmentEvents.desc: Showcase groundbreaking projects in a multi-disciplinary stage. Open to all branches.

departmentEvents.title: Seminar Competition
departmentEvents.subtitle: Sustainable Development in the 21st Century
departmentEvents.desc: Exploring innovative strategies and technologies driving sustainable development in the 21st century.

departments.name: Mechanical Engineering
departments.description: The Mechanical Engineering department brings raw engineering power to Tarang — from sheet metal fabrication and lathe machining to CAD mastery and 3D printing.

departmentEvents.title: Metal Fab Maestro
departmentEvents.subtitle: Sheet Metal Fabrication
departmentEvents.desc: A practical skill-based competition where participants fabricate a given sheet metal component using marking, cutting, bending, forming, and joining operations.

departmentEvents.title: MechCAD Master
departmentEvents.subtitle: AutoCAD Competition
departmentEvents.desc: Competition involving 2D and 3D machine component drawing in AutoCAD, adhering to BIS standards. Judged on accuracy, clarity, annotation, and assembly representation.

departmentEvents.title: Workshop on Additive Manufacturing
departmentEvents.subtitle: Innovate. Create. Fabricate.
departmentEvents.desc: A one-day workshop focused on additive manufacturing and 3D printing technologies.

departmentEvents.title: ProTurn
departmentEvents.subtitle: Machining Event
departmentEvents.desc: Participants perform lathe operations such as facing, straight turning, step turning, and taper turning to machine a component as per the provided drawing.

departmentEvents.title: MechIQ
departmentEvents.subtitle: Quiz Competition
departmentEvents.desc: A curriculum-based quiz for mechanical students covering core subjects to test technical knowledge, concepts, and problem-solving skills through time-bound rounds.

departmentEvents.title: Project Competition
departmentEvents.subtitle: Innovation Beyond Boundaries
departmentEvents.desc: An event highlighting interdepartmental engineering solutions. Open to all departments.

departmentEvents.title: Seminar Competition
departmentEvents.subtitle: Sustainable Development in the 21st Century
departmentEvents.desc: Exploring innovative strategies and technologies driving sustainable development in the 21st century.

departments.name: Textile Technology
departments.description: The Textile Technology department weaves creativity into Tarang with fashion shows, design competitions, and sustainable drape fusion events.

departmentEvents.title: Incredible Indian Fashion Show
departmentEvents.subtitle: Cultural Fashion Showcase
departmentEvents.desc: A showcase of Indian culture through fashion, where contestants are judged on original costumes and creative presentations.

departmentEvents.title: Fashion Sketch to Style
departmentEvents.subtitle: Fashion Illustration Contest
departmentEvents.desc: An on-site fashion illustration contest where participants create original fashion designs based on a specific theme.

departmentEvents.title: Pureform: A Sustainable Drape Fusion
departmentEvents.subtitle: Sustainable Design Competition
departmentEvents.desc: A design competition to create and drape an original outfit using only sustainable, recycled, or upcycled materials. The creation must be completed live.

departmentEvents.title: Design Decode
departmentEvents.subtitle: Brand & Logo Identification
departmentEvents.desc: A team event to identify brands, logos, and symbols within a specified time limit, testing observation and brand knowledge.

departmentEvents.title: Project Competition
departmentEvents.subtitle: Innovation Beyond Boundaries
departmentEvents.desc: Interdisciplinary project competition open to all branches showcasing innovative engineering solutions.

departmentEvents.title: Seminar Competition
departmentEvents.subtitle: Sustainable Development in the 21st Century
departmentEvents.desc: Exploring innovative strategies and technologies driving sustainable development in the 21st century.

creativeEvents.title: Photography Contest

Contestants should bring the required items

creativeEvents.desc: Transform transparent glass bottles into works of art. Theme given on the spot. Time duration: 2 hours. Only transparent bottles are permitted. Pre-painted bottles are not allowed.

Team of two members — artist and model

creativeEvents.title: Clay Modelling

creativeEvents.desc: Sculpt creative clay models using only clay and permitted materials. Model will be provided at the spot. Duration: 3 hours.

creativeEvents.title: Face Painting Contest
creativeEvents.subtitle: Paint the Face, Tell a Story
creativeEvents.desc: Transform faces into living art using skin-friendly paints. Team of two — artist and model. Candidates must bring their own skin-friendly paints.
rules: Skin friendly paints of own must be used by candidates
Team of two — artist and model

creativeEvents.desc: A live comical creation of a model provided at the spot. Pencils are the only permitted medium.

creativeEvents.subtitle: Street Art Unleashed
creativeEvents.desc: Express your street art skills on the allotted space. Participants should come with all required resources.

Use only the space allotted for the competition

culturalEvents.subtitle: Sing Your Heart Out
culturalEvents.desc: Mandatory live singing. Song duration: 4 to 8 minutes. Songs of any language can be performed.

culturalEvents.subtitle: Dance Battle Royale
culturalEvents.desc: Show your best moves in this high-energy dance battle. Duration: 3–7 minutes. Group size: min 3, max 15.

faq.question: Who can participate in Tarang 2026?
faq.answer: Tarang is primarily for students of technical education institutions (A valid college/any ID is required). Creative and Cultural events are open to public.

faq.question: When and where is Tarang 2026?
faq.answer: Tarang 2026 will be held on March 12 & 13, 2026 at the Government Polytechnic College Kannur campus.

faq.question: Is there a registration fee?
faq.answer: Yes, registration fees vary by event — typically ₹30 for quizzes and seminars, ₹50–₹100 for competitions, and ₹70–₹150 for workshops.

faq.question: Can I participate in multiple events?
faq.answer: Yes! You can participate in as many events as you want, as long as they don't overlap in schedule.

faq.question: Which departments host technical events?
faq.answer: All six departments — Civil, Electrical & Electronics, Electronics, Mechanical, Textile Technology, and Wood & Paper Technology — host their own technical events.

faq.question: Are Creative and Cultural events open to public?
faq.answer: Yes! All Creative events (Photography, Poster Design, Shorts/Reels, Glass Bottle Art, Mehndi, Clay Modelling, Face Painting, Caricature, Graffiti) and Cultural events (Karaoke, Break Dance) are open to the general public. No college ID is required.

faq.question: How are the events organized?
faq.answer: Events are spread across both days. Technical events include department-wise competitions. Creative & Cultural events are open to all and run in parallel. Workshops run on Day 2.
